visual_thesis: Carpenter is not a historical courtroom story first; it is the moment the invisible map created by an ordinary phone became constitutionally legible.

recurring_motifs.description: A blue/gold path of points blooming on a dark map; starts as viewer-present data, becomes legal evidence, then returns as the viewer's own device.
Phone pings to tower sectors, then connects into a route. Render in Remotion for accurate labels and count animation.
A narrow 1970s dialed-number list contrasted with a dense modern location trail.
A single electric-blue line crossing body, phone contents, and phone location; used for the trilogy payoff.
Doors labeled by data type, with location shown as the first door opened by Carpenter.

ai_visual_policy.disclosure: All generated or reconstructed visuals must be disclosed as AI-generated/symbolic reconstruction in the rights manifest and where context requires on-screen.
ai_visual_policy.no_likeness: Do not create an identifiable likeness of Timothy Carpenter, alleged confederates, officers, justices, store workers, or victims.
ai_visual_policy.no_authenticity_claim: Never imply generated scenes are authentic footage or documentary evidence.
ai_visual_policy.image_text_rule: Do not ask the image model to render readable text; exact text is added in Remotion.

camera_and_motion.stills: Cinematic documentary stills with restrained 2.5D parallax, shallow depth, deep contrast, fine film grain.
camera_and_motion.graphics: Remotion-first maps, diagrams, timelines, counters, lower thirds, chapter cards, vote graphics, and legal-standard comparisons.
camera_and_motion.motion: Avoid flashy tech UI. Favor slow map blooms, tower pings, measured line drawing, and precise typography.

prohibited_visuals: real-person portrait likeness of Timothy Carpenter
store logos such as RadioShack or T-Mobile unless separately cleared
graphic violence or weapons pointed at camera
fake court documents with readable hallucinated text
claiming any AI scene is archival or authentic footage
rights_and_qc_notes: Every AI still and local SDXL/SVD output must be registered in the rights manifest before edit assembly.
Every reenactment-style image requires symbolic reconstruction labeling and human review.
All text, numbers, citations, maps, and diagrams should be Remotion-rendered for accuracy.
The 5-4 vote and ~12,898 point count are high-sensitivity accuracy checks.

image_quality_bar.standard: Museum-grade cinematic stills only; reject generic AI-stock, decorative, distorted, or merely adequate outputs.
must_have: safe negative space for Remotion text
